description: Redesign the BurnBuddy web app with an Apple Fitness-inspired dark theme and responsive bottom navigation bar for mobile

userStories.title: Update global theme colors and typography
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to define the dark theme colors and typography globally so all components inherit the new design system.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/app/globals.css` @theme block with new color palette: --color-primary: #30D158 (Apple Fitness green), --color-surface: #1C1C1E (dark card background), --color-surface-elevated: #2C2C2E (elevated dark elements), --color-secondary: #0A84FF (bright blue), --color-accent-pink: #FF2D55 (active workout indicators). Keep --color-danger: #ef4444
Update `apps/web/tailwind.config.ts` with matching color definitions
Set body background to black (#000), default text to white in globals.css
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update root layout for dark theme and bottom nav spacing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update the root layout to apply dark theme globally and add padding for the mobile bottom navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx` body classes: `min-h-screen bg-black text-white antialiased`
Add `pb-20 md:pb-0` to body or main wrapper to accommodate mobile bottom nav (20 = 5rem spacing)
Move `<NavBar />` into layout.tsx (above children) to render once globally
Create separate route group for login/signup pages without NavBar (e.g., `(auth)` group)
Typecheck passes
Verify layout renders correctly using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Redesign NavBar as responsive top/bottom navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a modern navigation bar that's optimized for my device - top bar on desktop, bottom tabs on mobile - so navigation feels native to each platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/components/NavBar.tsx` with responsive design: Desktop (`hidden md:flex`): Horizontal top bar, dark background, logo left, Home/Friends/Account links right, Sign Out button. Mobile (`md:hidden`): Fixed bottom bar (`fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 z-50`), dark background with subtle top border
Mobile: Three icon tabs for Home (/), Friends (/friends), Account (/account) using simple inline SVG icons
Active tab highlighted with green color (#30D158) and pill-shaped background
Sign Out button only on desktop; mobile users access it via Account page
Remove `<NavBar />` imports from all page components (layout now handles it)
Typecheck passes
Verify navigation on mobile and desktop using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark theme home page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the home page styled with the dark theme so it matches the Apple Fitness aesthetic and is easy on my eyes.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/app/page.tsx` with dark theme styling: DashboardSkeleton with `bg-gray-800` placeholders, Start Workout button green (`bg-primary hover:bg-green-600`), Active Workout banner dark card (`bg-surface`) with accent-pink border/text, Workout type selector modal dark background (`bg-surface`) with green active state
Buddy/squad list items: `bg-surface` cards, white text, `border-gray-700` instead of light borders
Burn streak emoji 🔥 color: green text instead of orange
Pending request sections: dark cards (`bg-surface`) with subtle colored borders
Section headers: `text-white` instead of `text-gray-500`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark theme StatCard component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the StatCard component styled for dark theme so all stats displays look consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/components/StatCard.tsx`: Background `bg-surface` instead of `bg-white`, Border `border-gray-700` instead of `border-gray-200`, Labels `text-gray-400`, keep colorClass logic
Typecheck passes
Verify StatCard appearance using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark theme GettingStartedCard component
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Getting Started card styled with dark theme so it blends with the home page design.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/components/GettingStartedCard.tsx`: Replace `bg-green-50` with `bg-surface`, Replace `border-slate-200` with `border-gray-700`, text white/light gray, Strong tags `text-primary` (green), Close button `text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-200`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark theme Account page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Account page styled with dark theme so I can manage my profile in a consistent, modern interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/app/account/page.tsx`: All section cards `bg-surface border-gray-700`, headings `text-white`, descriptions `text-gray-400`, input fields `bg-surface-elevated border-gray-600 text-white placeholder-gray-500`, buttons green primary/red danger/gray secondary with dark styling, AccountSkeleton `bg-gray-800` placeholders, sign-out section dark card with red accent border
Remove `import { NavBar }` and `<NavBar />` from account page (layout handles it)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark theme Friends page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Friends page styled with dark theme so I can manage my friend list in a modern, easy-to-read interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `apps/web/src/app/friends/page.tsx`: Search input `bg-surface-elevated border-gray-600 text-white placeholder-gray-500`, friend list items `bg-surface border-gray-700` cards, section headings `text-white`, buttons green/blue on dark backgrounds, FriendsSkeleton `bg-gray-800`, empty states `text-gray-400`
Remove `import { NavBar }` and `<NavBar />` from friends page (layout handles it)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark theme for Burn Buddy and Squad detail/create pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want all Burn Buddy and Burn Squad pages styled with dark theme for visual consistency throughout the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Update all detail and create pages: `apps/web/src/app/burn-buddies/[id]/page.tsx`, `apps/web/src/app/burn-buddies/new/page.tsx`, `apps/web/src/app/burn-squads/[id]/page.tsx`, `apps/web/src/app/burn-squads/new/page.tsx`, `apps/web/src/app/profile/[uid]/page.tsx`

Apply consistent dark styling: Cards `bg-surface border-gray-700`, Headings `text-white`, Body text `text-gray-300`, Secondary text `text-gray-400`, Inputs `bg-surface-elevated border-gray-600 text-white`, Buttons green primary/colored secondary/gray tertiary, Skeletons `bg-gray-800`
Remove NavBar imports from all pages (layout handles it)
Typecheck passes
Verify all 5 pages in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dark theme for Login and Signup pages with Firebase widget override
userStories.description: As a user, I want the login and signup pages styled with dark theme, including the Firebase authentication widget, so the auth flow feels consistent with the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create route group `apps/web/src/app/(auth)` with its own `layout.tsx` (no NavBar)
Move `login/page.tsx` and `signup/page.tsx` into `(auth)` group
Update login/signup pages: dark background (inherits from body), white heading text, gray body text

Add Firebase Auth widget CSS overrides in `globals.css`: Override `.firebaseui-container` background to dark, `.firebaseui-card-content` to `bg-surface`, text colors to white/gray, input fields dark (`bg-surface-elevated`, white text), buttons to match BurnBuddy styles
Typecheck passes
Verify login and signup flows in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: May need !important to override Firebase widget's inline/scoped styles.
